dto.creator: achriston

testSectionDTOList.descriptionGerman:
• Set up the Standard Neonatal Environment as shown in Chapter 2.1
  • Ensure, all devices are running and connected to the Network
  • Ensure, that the TAI-Server application is running on the notebook connected to the ASL-5000
  • Start the device
  • Enable the magic key "Neo APV Plimit 100mbar".
  • If not already done run all calibrations and tests in the “Preop check”-tab with the correct humidification type
  • Set the SBLVM to “off”.
  • Set the leakage valve to 20% (±5%) by using Vti and Vte of the IMT Flow Analyser (Vte = 0.8 * Vti). You can run “Vent Mode: Set Leakage for Neonatal” on https://medical-portal.hamilton.ch/apps/ait-mgmt/testCases/66/revisions/0 to set the leakage valve.
  • Run the last released version of the test case “[STBR] PPL.0003 Ventilation Mode (VS) neonatal automatic FULL” with the ID 109 on Medical Portal: AIT Management.
  • After the test is executed, export the test results and copy them into the tables of the test-case.
testSectionDTOList.title: Test Preparation

testSectionDTOList.descriptionGerman:
In this test, the applied PEEP and tidal volume are tested. For each partial test, the sequence is:

(ASL)->(Setup)->(Exec)->(Scripting)->(Evaluation)->(CleanUp)

(ASL)-Block: Sets ASL resistance and compliance according to the product requirements.

(Setup)-Block: Sets predefined values according to the product requirements.

(Exec)-Block:

  • Starts ventilation.
  • Waits for 90 seconds.
  • Stores two measurements of Vti and Vte from the ventilator in the variables [VTE_VENTILATOR_1], [VTI_VENTILATOR_1], [VTE_VENTILATOR_2], [VTI_VENTILATOR_2]
  • Stores one measurement of Vti and Vte read from the Flow-Analyser in [VTE_FLOWANALYSER], [VTI_FLOWANALYSER].
  • The block takes two measurements from the ventilator, in case the first one is taken while the Controller is auto-zeroing, resulting in an invalid measurement.

(Scripting)-Block: 

  • Checks the ventilator measurements from the (Exec)-block for invalid values and substitutes invalid values by a spare measurement if necessary.
  • Calculates VT from the ventilator and stores it in the variable [VT_VENTILATOR].
  • Calculates VT from the flow-analyser and stores it in the variable [VT_FLOWANALYSER].
    Note: The value in [VT_VENTILATOR] is stored in microliter.

(Evaluation)-Block:

  • Evaluates PEEP measured by the ventilator and by the flow-analyser
  • Evaluates the calculated VT in [VT_FLOWANALYSER] and in [VT_VENTILATOR]
  • Evaluates the Peak-Pressure by the ventilator and by the flow-analyser for low Target-volumes

(CleanUp)-Block: Stops ventilation.
testSectionDTOList.title: Tidal Volume and PEEP

testElementDTOList.targetRawValue: Set VTI_VENTILATOR to a valid capture
VTI_VENTILATOR = VTI_VENTILATOR_1 if VTI_VENTILATOR_1 > 0 else VTI_VENTILATOR_2;

VTE_VENTILATOR = VTE_VENTILATOR_1 if VTE_VENTILATOR_1 > 0 else VTE_VENTILATOR_2;

  • Measured PPeak > Target-Peep + 5mbar (i.e. Pinsp of APV-controller > 5mbar)
  • Measured Peep = Target-Peep 
  • Measured Volume = Target-Volume

testSectionDTOList.descriptionGerman:
In this test, the applied inspiration pressure and the applied PEEP of patient-triggered breaths are tested when Plimit is at the maximum value of 100mbar.  It verifies that the maximum applied pressure is 100mbar, limited by "Pressure limitation". The sequence is:

(ASL)->(Setup)->(Exec)->(Evaluation)->(CleanUp)

(ASL)-Block: Sets inspiration pressure, breath reate, resistance and compliance of the ASL.

(Setup)-Block: Sets control settings and the upper Pmax alarm limit.

(Exec)-Block:

  • Starts ventilation.
  • Waits a certain time to reach steady-state.

(Evaluation)-Block:

  • Asserts that the "Pressure limitation" alarm is present.
  • Evaluates PEEP measured by the ventilator and by the flow-analyser
  • Evaluates the Peak-Pressure by the ventilator and by the flow-analyser

(CleanUp)-Block: Stops ventilation.
testSectionDTOList.title: ........Patient-triggered breaths with VT = max, Plimit = max {C6}

................(Setup)Patient-triggered breaths with VT = max, Plimit = max {C6}
................(Exec)Patient-triggered breaths with VT = max, Plimit = max {C6}

................(CleanUp)Patient-triggered breaths with VT = max, Plimit = max {C6}

testSectionDTOList.descriptionGerman:
In this test, the minimal applied pressure by the ventilator is tested. The test is passed, if the applied peak pressure is 5 mbar (minimal inspiration pressure) above the set PEEP, and the applied tidal volume is higher than the set target volume. The sequence for this test is:

(ASL)->(Setup)->(Exec)->(Scripting)->(Evaluation)->(CleanUp)

(ASL)-Block: Sets ASL resistance and compliance according to the product requirements.

(Setup)-Block: Sets predefined values according to the product requirements.

(Exec)-Block:

  • Starts ventilation.
  • Waits for 90 seconds.
  • Stores one measurement of the inspiration and expiration volume measured by the flow-analyser in the variables [VTI_FLOWANALYSER] and [VTE_FLOWANALYSER].

(Scripting)-Block: Calculates the applied tidal volume and stores it in [VT_FLOWANALYSER]

(Evaluation)-Block:

  • Evaluates the applied peak pressure measured by the flow-analyser.
  • Evaluates the applied tidal volume stored in [VT_FLOWANALYSER]

(CleanUp)-Block: Stops ventilation.

testSectionDTOList.descriptionGerman:
This test shows that the inspiration phase time of a mandaotory patient-triggered breath is terminated by TI max if the ETS-condition is not yet fullfilled. The sequence is:

(Setup) -> (Exec |1/2) -> (ASL | 1/2) -> (Exec |2/2) ->(Evaluation) -> (CleanUp)

(Setup)-Block: Sets the ventilator settings incl. ETS = 5%, TI Max = 1.8 s and the upper alarm limit of Vt = “off”.

(Exec |1/2)-Block:

  • Starts ventilation.
  • Waits 20s.

(ASL | 2/2)-Block: Ends the simulation. Note: When the ASL ends the simulation, the SBLVM is fully opened. Since no balloon lung is attached to the SBLVM, this is equivalent to disconnecting the test lung and causes auto-triggering.

(Exec |2/2)-Block: Waits 45s to reach steady-state of autotriggering (chain of detected false-positive patient-triggered breaths).

(Evaluation)-Block: 

  • Evaluates the applied nspiration time by the ventilator and by the IMT Flow Analyser.

(CleanUp)-Block:

  • Stops ventilation.
  • Restarts the ASL simulation.
testSectionDTOList.title: TinspMax

testSectionDTOList.descriptionGerman:
In this test, the minimal applied expiration time is tested. For each partial test, the sequence is:

(ASL)->(Setup)->(Exec)->(Scripting)->(Evaluation)->(CleanUp)

(ASL)-Block: Sets ASL resistance and compliance according to the product requirements.

(Setup)-Block: Sets predefined values according to the product requirements.

(Exec)-Block:

  • Starts ventilation.
  • Waits for a given time interval for the ventilator tor each steady state.
  • Stores one measurement of the expiration time measured by the Flow-Analyser.

(Scripting)-Block: Converts the measurement from the Flow-Analyzer from “s” to “ms”. Note: The value is multiplied due to insufficient capabilities of AIT to evaluate floating-point numbers.

(Evaluation)-Block: Compares the measurement to the expected value.

(CleanUp)-Block: Stops ventilation.
testSectionDTOList.title: TexpMin

testSectionDTOList.descriptionGerman:
All patient efforts are detected. This shows that the expiration phase of a spontaneous patient-triggered breath can be terminated after 0.2 s. 

Here, the defined ASL-settings lead the applied inspiration phase time.
testSectionDTOList.title: ........TexpMin with patient effort Texp=0.2

testElementDTOList.targetRawValue: If first measurement is taken, while trigger was not detected, take the second measurement
Factor Expiration time by a factor of 1000

testSectionDTOList.descriptionGerman:
Only each 2nd patient effort is detected. This shows that patient efforts inside the TexpMin-window are ineffective. The expected applied expiration phase time is 1.1 s.

Here, the defined ASL-settings lead the applied inspiration phase time.
testSectionDTOList.title: ........TexpMin with ineffective patient efforts Texp = 1.1s

testElementDTOList.targetRawValue: Factor Expiration time by a factor of 1000

testSectionDTOList.descriptionGerman:
In this test, the applied time of the minimal-inspiration state of a ETS-controlled spontaneous breath is tested. TinspMin is equal to 0.1s.

Tinsp is calculated based on Default-C6-Rate (=45b/min for Weight = 2kg) and Default-C6-I:E (= 1:3). This leads to a Tinsp of  0.33s

The sequence is:

(ASL)->(Setup)->(Exec)->(Scripting)->(Evaluation)->(CleanUp)

(ASL)-Block: Sets the ASL to have appropriate patient breathing. The spontaneous breath should be as short as possible, such that it switches to expiration phase exactly after TinspMin. 

(Setup)-Block:

  • Sets the ventilator settings including ETS = 80 %.

(Exec)-Block:

  • Starts ventilation.
  • Waits 15s.
  • Records the measured inspiration time of two breaths read by the Ventilator and the Flow Analyser.

(Scripting)-Block:

  • Takes the measurement with the lower value.
  • Compares the measurement to the expected value of TinspMin + 10ms
  • Store the result of the comparison

(Evaluation)-Block: Evaluates the result

(CleanUp)-Block: Stops ventilation.
testSectionDTOList.title: TinspMin
........TinspMin with patient effort ETS = 80% and Tinsp = 0.33s

................(Exec)TinspMin with patient effort ETS = 80% and Tinsp = 0.33s

V_TI= V_TI_2 if V_TI > V_TI_2 else V_TI
